Services Case Studies Work Posts Careers Why RePass Cloud Start a conversation
Services

Cloud Infrastructure Tooling

Cost analysis, automation, and operational tooling for organisations running workloads across Azure, AWS, and hybrid cloud environments.

The cloud was supposed to make infrastructure simpler. For most organisations running at scale, the reality is a sprawl of accounts, regions, services, and teams — each with their own tooling, their own cost centres, and their own operational practices. The complexity didn't go away; it moved.

RePass Cloud builds tooling that gives you control over that complexity. Not another dashboard bolted on top of existing dashboards — actual automation that reduces manual operational work, actual visibility that surfaces real cost and risk, and actual integration with the Microsoft ecosystem your organisation already depends on.

Common problems

What we're usually solving

Cloud bill growing faster than usage
No visibility across multiple cloud accounts
Infrastructure provisioned manually with no audit trail
Certificate expiry causing unplanned outages
Different tooling per cloud provider with no unified view
Ops team spending too much time on repetitive tasks
What we deliver

Cloud tooling capabilities

Cloud cost analysis & FinOps

Unified cost visibility across AWS, Azure, and GCP. We build tooling that surfaces where spend is going, tracks savings realisation, identifies waste, and gives finance and engineering teams a shared view of cloud economics.

SSL certificate management

Automated certificate provisioning, renewal tracking, and expiry alerting across multi-cloud and hybrid environments. Eliminate the manual spreadsheet chase and the 2am "site down" call because a cert expired.

VM & compute automation

Lifecycle management for virtual machines across Azure and AWS — provisioning, scaling, patching scheduling, snapshot management, and decommission workflows — all codified and repeatable.

Infrastructure as code & templating

Bicep, ARM, Terraform, and PowerShell DSC for repeatable, auditable infrastructure. We write IaC that is maintainable by the team that inherits it, not just the engineer who wrote it.

Monitoring, alerting & observability

Telemetry pipelines, log aggregation, and alerting built around what your operations team actually needs to know — not just what the cloud provider dashboard shows by default.

Operational runbooks & automation

Convert manual runbooks into automated workflows. Routine operational tasks — backups, health checks, environment resets, access reviews — should not require a human to open a console.

Integration

Cloud tooling in the Microsoft ecosystem

Most of the organisations we work with are Microsoft-first: Azure for compute and storage, Entra ID for identity, Microsoft 365 for productivity, and Intune or Configuration Manager for endpoint management. Cloud tooling doesn't operate in isolation from this — it needs to integrate with it.

That means cost tooling that maps spend to actual Entra ID groups and departmental owners, not just resource tags. It means VM automation that respects the same permission model as the rest of your environment. It means certificate management that works with Azure Key Vault and integrates with your existing IT service management workflow.

Where AWS or multi-cloud workloads exist alongside Azure, we build the unified layer that gives visibility across both — without requiring two separate operating models.

Case Study · Enterprise Technology

Multi-Cloud FinOps Tooling

Unified cost visibility across a $7M USD/year cloud estate spanning AWS, Azure, and GCP. We built a savings realisation tracking and governance platform that gave finance and engineering a single view of cloud economics across all providers.

AWS Azure GCP FinOps
